rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce The Settlement Agreement

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS NUSSBAUM VS. CAL INC.,A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Re Notice) Of Motion For Court To Define Terms Of Settlement Agreement; And Other Defts To Submit The Proper Documents To Fbop As The Gsa Contract Holder
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 10, 2010, line 1, PLAINTIFF THOMAS NUSSBAUM (Re Notice) of Motion for Court to Define Terms of Settlement Agreement; and Other Defendants to Submit the Proper Documents To FBOP as The GSA Contract Holder. Motion continued to March 3, 2010. Parties must meet and confer regarding the actions they will take in the event the claim is rejected or ignored. By February 26, 2010, parties must file a joint status report discribing further actions taken and progress made in resolving the change order issue, and specifying the parties duties and responsibilities in the event the claim is rejected or ignored. If the parties cannot agree regarding their duties and responsibilities, their respective positions will be set forth in the joint statement. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT KEHOE et al VS. PATRICK HENNESSY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 10, 2010, line 18, DEFENDANTS UNITED CABINET OUTLET, INC., RAYMOND ZHANG MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Motion granted. Defendants to pay plaintiffs' attorney fees and costs in obtaining the defaults. (CCP section 473.) Fees and costs will be the subject of a separately filed motion if the parties do not stipulate to the amount. Request for additonal sanctions denied. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREAS AMUNDIN VS. METHOD360, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPOPRATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Sanctions; Req. For Judicial Ntc And Appendix Of Foreign Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 10, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANT MCKESSON CORPORATION Motion For Sanctions. Motion denied. No showing that CCP section 128.7(b) has been violated. Issue of whether an individual is an employee or an independent contractor is a highly factual issue. Mere discovery reponses that suggest one catorization over another do not necessarily prove that contradictory allegations pleaded in a subsequent case were made for an improper purpose. Also, to the extent defendant relies on discovery responses in the Blackstone litigation, sanctions may not be awarded. CCP section 128.7(g). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: AUGUSTINE FALLAY VS. CRYSTAL LEI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 10, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT TONY FU MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Motion to strike granted. The first cause of action for breach of contract and the third cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress are based on the prior lawsuit and defendant's report to the FBI/DA. That conduct implicates petitioning activity under CCP section 425.16(e). Plaintiff fails to demonstrate a liklihood of prevailing. The contract lacks consideration and is barred by the Statute of Frauds. The emotional distress claim arose in 2005, but plaintiff waited until June 2009 to bring this case. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: AUGUSTINE FALLAY VS. CRYSTAL LEI et al

rulings.Case Title: AUGUSTINE FALLAY VS. CRYSTAL LEI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 10, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT CRYSTAL LEI MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Motion to strike granted. The first cause of action for breach of contract and the third cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress are based on the prior lawsuit and defendant's report to the FBI/DA. That conduct implicates petitioning activity under CCP section 425.16(e). Plaintiff fails to demonstrate a liklihood of prevailing. The contract lacks consideration and is barred by the Statute of Frauds. The emotional distress claim arose in 2005, but plaintiff waited until June 2009 to bring this case. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: AUGUSTINE FALLAY VS. CRYSTAL LEI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 10, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT CRYSTAL LEI DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer moot as to first and third causes of action. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to second cause of action for fraud. The complaint alleges defendants engaged in the fraud to get plaintiff prosecuted. Plaintiff was charged in 2005, and therefore plaintiff knew of the fraud at that time. The cause of action is time-barred. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ARUNDEL HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. ARUNDEL GREEN PARTNERS,A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 10, 2010, line 7, DEFENDANT ZAKSKOM CONSTRUCTION COMPANY DBA ZCON BUILDERS DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. CCP section 337.15 bars this claim. The Certificate of Final Completion was issued 4/28/98. ZCON never entered into any agreement to toll the statute of limitations. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. =(302/CWW)
